description:
You are a junior backup-operator performing a quick “dry-run” restore.  
    A plain-text catalogue of every file inside last night’s archive is already on disk:

        /home/user/backups/backup_filelist.txt

    Your goal is to create a *restore-preview* list that contains **only** those catalogued
    paths whose file-name actually ends with the literal extension “.conf”.

    Requirements for the preview list:
    1. Store it at the exact path  
          /home/user/restores/preview_to_restore.lst  
       (create the /home/user/restores directory if it does not exist).
    2. The file must contain nothing but the selected, absolute paths, one per line, in
       the same order they appear in backup_filelist.txt.
    3. Do **not** add any header, footer, extra blank lines, or surrounding whitespace.
       The very last line must still be terminated with a single trailing newline
       character (Unix “\n”).
    4. Complete the task using a single terminal command.

    When you are finished, the automated test will:
      • Check that /home/user/restores/preview_to_restore.lst exists.  
      • Verify that it is world-readable (at least 0644).  
      • Confirm its contents match the exact set of “.conf” lines, in order, with a
        trailing newline and no other text.

    Hint: the simplest way is to combine an appropriate text-filtering command with
    output redirection.
truth:
Initial filesystem objects that already exist before the agent starts:

    1. Directory: /home/user/backups  (permissions 0755)
    2. File: /home/user/backups/backup_filelist.txt  (permissions 0644)
       Exact contents (Unix LF line endings, final newline present):

            /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
            /etc/ssh/sshd_config
            /var/www/html/index.html
            /etc/mysql/my.cnf
            /home/user/.bashrc
            /etc/supervisor/supervisord.conf
            /var/log/syslog
            /etc/httpd/conf/httpd.conf

       (That is eight lines, shown above, each ending with ‘\n’.)

    Objects the agent must create:

    1. Directory: /home/user/restores  (permissions 0755 if created by agent)
    2. File: /home/user/restores/preview_to_restore.lst  (permissions 0644)
       Required contents (exactly three lines, final newline present):

            /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
            /etc/supervisor/supervisord.conf
            /etc/httpd/conf/httpd.conf

    The automated test will check that preview_to_restore.lst contains the three
    lines shown above, in that order, with no additional lines, spaces, or characters.
